Borehole is situated directly on Murtel rock glacier about 30 m downslope from borehole COR_0200. At the moment only aggregated 24 h values are stored in the DB and the data for several depths are not completely included in the DB, since these data seems to be corrupted. The uppermost thermistor temperatures are theoretically also available with 6 h interval prior to the renovation, and since the renovation all thermistor temperatures are measured every 2 h. The thermistor chain is stuck and could not be recalibrated since 1997.;Some additional information about the borehole instrumentation and further references can be found in Arenson (2002): Unstable alpine permafrost: A potentially important natural hazard - Variations of geotechnical behaviour with time and temperature. PhD, ETH Zurich, 97-108.

permos_alt_comment: large thermistor spacing, not comparable to A

permos_borehole_comment: PACE borehole, prior to the renovation in summer 2011, the uppermost thermistor temperatures are also available in 6h intervals (data not in DB and never properly analyzed).
permos_site_comment: Debris-covered E-W oriented crest. Most measurement installations in the N slope about 60 m below the summit, but some GST measurements also further down as well as in the S slope. Further information can be found e.g. in J. Noetzli, C. Hilbich, C. Hauck, M. Hoelzle, and S. Gruber (2008): Comparison of simulated 2D temperature profiles with time-lapse electrical resistivity data at the Schilthorn crest, Switzerland. In 9th International Conference on Permafrost. Fairbanks, US, 1293-1298.

Noetzli, C. Hilbich, C. Hauck, M. Hoelzle, and S. Gruber (2008): Comparison of simulated 2D temperature profiles with time-lapse electrical resistivity data at the Schilthorn crest, Switzerland. In 9th International Conference on Permafrost. Fairbanks, US, 1293-1298.
